entities.name: Multi-step orchestration
entities.context: Architectural approach using multiple steps with simpler models for better system behavior

entities.name: Model asymmetry
entities.context: Pattern of using different capability models for different tasks, enabling granular error correction

entities.name: Chain of Thought architecture
entities.context: Architecture that reduces cognitive load on single inference passes by distributing complexity

entities.name: Multi-Agent architecture
entities.context: Related pattern to Chain of Thought for distributing complexity across specialized agents

entities.name: Single-shot prompts
entities.context: Approach of using one prompt to high-end models, contrasted unfavorably with orchestration

entities.name: Frontier models
entities.context: High-capability models that V believes should not be relied upon for complex reasoning

entities.name: Hallucination
entities.context: Failure mode in large monolithic models attempting complex reasoning in one pass

entities.name: 4o-mini
entities.context: Example of a small model that can be tuned for specific mechanical tasks

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company whose architecture should bias toward complex workflows with cheaper models

relationships.context: Multi-step orchestration enables model asymmetry pattern
Model asymmetry allows for granular error correction and specialized execution
Both are examples of distributing complexity to raise system output ceiling
Large monolithic models are prone to hallucination when attempting complex reasoning
Careerspan's architecture should bias toward complex workflows using cheaper, faster models
Small models like 4o-mini can be tuned for specific mechanical tasks in orchestrated workflows
